items.title: At least 90 killed in Chinese coal mine explosion, state media reports
items.summary: The explosion took place at 19:29 local time on Friday at a coal mine in northern China.

items.title: Putin vows retaliation after accusing Ukraine of hitting student dormitory
items.summary: Ukraine says it struck Russia's elite Rubicon drone military unit in a Moscow-occupied region.

items.title: SpaceX launches massive Starship V3 rocket on test flight
items.summary: The largest and most powerful rocket in history blasted off after its first attempted launch was postponed.

items.title: Rubio tries to reassure Nato allies over US troop deployments
items.summary: President Donald Trump says he wants to send more troops to Poland, a week after his officials cancelled a similar deployment.

items.title: Ebola risk raised to 'very high' in DR Congo
items.summary: The head of the UN health agency says the risk in the wider region is "high", but it remains "low" at the global level.

items.title: Race for French presidency sees ex-PM Philippe as early favourite to beat populists
items.summary: Latest polls suggest the centre-right figure is the only candidate who can defeat Marine Le Pen or Jean-Luc Mélenchon.

items.title: Tulsi Gabbard to resign as US national intelligence director
items.summary: Gabbard who has been largely out of public view during recent US operations says she is leaving due to her husband's illness.

items.title: Senegal's president sacks prime minister Sonko after months of tensions
items.summary: Bassirou Diomaye Faye also dissolved the government, creating uncertainty amid a debt crisis in the country.

items.title: Gaza flotilla activists allege abuse by Israeli forces while detained
items.summary: The detainees say they were slapped, hit, humiliated and even subjected to sexual violence, but Israel's prison service says the allegations are false.

items.title: Rubio visits India to sell energy as Iran oil shock persists
items.summary: The top US diplomat has made statements offering to support India’s energy needs - but should Delhi give in?

items.title: US government releases UFO sighting reports - 'Orbs swarming in all directions'
items.summary: The Pentagon discloses scores of UFO files with reported sightings of spheres, discs and fireballs spanning nearly 80 years.

items.title: One dead and dozens of firefighters injured in Staten Island shipyard explosion
items.summary: More than 30 people, including firefighters, were hurt in the incident, which was described as "a complex, fast-developing emergency situation".

items.title: Bodies of Italian divers did not have optimal equipment, says rescuer
items.summary: Images from a cave where the four Italians were found reveal the difficulty that rescuers had in recovering their bodies.

items.title: More than 40,000 Californians evacuated due to chemical tank leak
items.summary: Officials have warned that the tank contains a "highly volatile" substance that could lead to an explosion.

items.title: Carney says Alberta is 'essential' to Canada as province plans vote on separation
items.summary: Alberta's decision to hold a referendum on separation has been widely criticised, including by separatists who say the question is not definitive enough.

items.title: Judge dismisses criminal case against Kilmar Abrego Garcia
items.summary: A federal judge says the Maryland man who was wrongfully deported last year is being prosecuted for political reasons.

items.title: Sabalenka cuts media short as French Open players protest
items.summary: Leading players, including Aryna Sabalenka, Coco Gauff and Jannik Sinner, limit their French Open media duties to 15 minutes in a row over prize money.

items.title: Waymo pauses robotaxis in five US cities after cars drive into flooded roads
items.summary: A Waymo spokesperson said it had expanded the temporary pause "out of an abundance of caution".

items.title: 'Speed, money and compassion' - lessons from an Ebola survivor and other experts
items.summary: Those caught up in West Africa's Ebola outbreak a decade ago on how best to tackle the current epidemic.

items.title: The space race to create gym equipment for future astronauts
items.summary: Scientists are attempting to build exercise equipment to be used on future space flights.

items.title: Trump is putting pressure on Cuba - why and to what end?
items.summary: The US has warned that a peaceful agreement with Cuba is unlikely, Havana insists this is a "fraudulent case" designed to justify military intervention.

items.title: An Indian bride dies. Rival claims of murder and suicide set off media frenzy
items.summary: Twisha Sharma, a model and actor, had been married for just five months when she was found dead.

items.title: Iran steps up claim to control Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: Iran publishes a map claiming "armed forces oversight" across more than 22,000 sq km of the waterway.

items.title: 'No means no': Greenlanders protest against Trump outside new US consulate
items.summary: The inauguration of the new consulate comes amid a push by the US president for greater control over the island.

items.title: Moment SpaceX rocket explodes in the Indian Ocean after splashdown
items.summary: Elon Musk's SpaceX has successfully launched its Starship V3 rocket, which landed in a planned fiery explosion on Friday.

items.title: Why thousands of stock trades tied to Trump are raising eyebrows
items.summary: The BBC's Michelle Fleury looks at trades disclosed by the president.

items.title: Timelapse footage shows 'giant cave' inflating on Paris bridge
items.summary: The monumental structure was created by Parisan artist JR - often dubbed the French Banksy

items.title: How Cuban-Americans feel about Raúl Castro's indictment
items.summary: The DOJ charged the former leader of Cuba with murder and other crimes for his alleged role in the downing of two civilian aircraft in 1996.

items.title: Fans react to Stephen Colbert’s final show
items.summary: The late-night host will appear behind his CBS desk for the final time after the network announced last summer that the show would end after 11 seasons.

items.title: Watch: 'Everybody back up!' Moment car explodes in NYC
items.summary: Viral footage circulating online shows flames and thick black smoke enveloping the street in New York.
